name: PageHeader
library: karoo-ui / MUI Stack — real fleet-web implementation (not an MDC spec)
source: apps/fleet-web/src/components/_containers/PageHeader/index.tsx (real fleet-web source, 43 usages confirmed 2026-07-23 — no md2-cartrack-library spec exists for this one; see AppBar's removal)
description: The horizontal row at the top of almost every authenticated page — title (or a back-link) on the left, page-level actions on the right. Unlike most of this library, it isn't modeled on an idealized MD2 spec: it's a direct, faithful port of the real, currently-shipping fleet-web component, not an approximation.
keyRule: Use PageHeader at the top of any page. Put PageHeader.Title (or a Breadcrumbs-based back-link) first and PageHeader.ButtonsContainer second — the real component has no other slots or props, so don't invent a subtitle, icon, or backLink prop; compose those from other components as children instead.

variants.name: Simple (title only)
variants.whenToUse: Settings-style pages with no page-level action — saving happens per section (e.g. My Profile, Audit).

variants.name: List (title + actions)
variants.whenToUse: Collection pages: title plus one or more primary/secondary Buttons in ButtonsContainer (e.g. Vehicles, Drivers, Geofences — "Add X").

variants.name: Detail (back-link + actions)
variants.whenToUse: Drill-down pages: the left slot becomes a back-link (Breadcrumbs > back arrow + parent label > current record name) instead of a plain Title; ButtonsContainer still holds page actions.

anatomy.name: Left slot
anatomy.description: Title (an h5 heading) on most pages, or a Breadcrumbs-based back-link + record name on detail pages.

anatomy.name: ButtonsContainer
anatomy.description: right-aligned, wrapping row for one or more page-level actions — Buttons, or a Menu trigger for overflow.

behaviors.name: Layout
behaviors.description: A single flex row, `justify-content: space-between` — the two slots never overlap; ButtonsContainer wraps onto a second line on narrow viewports instead of overflowing.

behaviors.name: Scrolling
behaviors.description: No sticky/fixed positioning, no elevation, no border — the header scrolls with the page. Don't add any of these when applying this component unless a specific page has a documented reason to.

behaviors.name: Composition with Tabs
behaviors.description: Tabs (and their divider/border) always render in their own row below PageHeader, never inside it.

states.treatment: The only state PageHeader itself has — it's purely a layout container. Interactive states (hover/focus/disabled) belong to whatever Buttons, links, or Menu triggers are placed inside it.

props.name: children
props.description: PageHeader itself: put PageHeader.Title (or a back-link) first and PageHeader.ButtonsContainer second.

props.name: PageHeader.Title — children
props.description: The page title text. Renders as a real <h5> — keep it as the page's one main heading.

props.name: PageHeader.ButtonsContainer — children
props.description: One or more page-level actions (Buttons, a Menu trigger). No count limit is enforced, but see dontDoThis.

doThis: Put Title (or a back-link) in the left slot, ButtonsContainer in the right — that order, every time.
On detail pages, build the back-link from Breadcrumbs (back arrow + parent label) followed by the record name, not a bare IconButton.
Keep it borderless, elevation-less, and non-sticky — that's what every real usage does.
dontDoThis: Don't add a subtitle, icon, or backLink prop — the real component doesn't have one; compose it from children instead.
Don't put Tabs, a divider, or a border-bottom inside PageHeader — that row belongs directly below it.
Don't make it sticky or elevated — no real usage does this; if a page seems to need it, flag the gap rather than inventing the behavior here.
Don't use more than one contained primary Button in ButtonsContainer — same one-primary-action rule as everywhere else in this library.
accessibility: Title renders as a real <h5> — don't wrap it in another heading level, and don't skip it as the page's one primary heading.
A detail-page back-link is a real link (or button) with an accessible name that includes where it goes back to, not just an unlabeled arrow icon.
Every action in ButtonsContainer needs the same accessible-name and visible-focus treatment as Button/Menu already document.

related.name: Breadcrumbs
related.why: Repurposed for the detail-page back-link variant (back arrow + parent label + record name)

related.name: Button
related.why: The primary/secondary actions that live inside ButtonsContainer

related.name: Menu
related.why: Overflow actions inside ButtonsContainer when there are more than fit as visible buttons

related.name: Tabs
related.why: Always sits in its own row directly below PageHeader, never inside it
